channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kitale Mourns Popular Biker Gustavo Rides Following Tragic Northern Bypass Accident
item.description: In Kitale, tearful scenes marked the burial of Elvis Juma, popularly known as Gustavo Rides or Luhya Kidd, who tragically died alongside his wife Diana in a motorcycle accident on the Northern Bypass....

item.title: Deontay Wilder edges Chisora and eyes long-awaited fight with Anthony Joshua
item.description: Deontay Wilder reignited calls for a long-awaited clash with fellow former heavyweight champion Anthony Joshua after securing a hard-fought split-decision win over Derek Chisora in London on Saturday....

item.title: Developments in the 2026 Iran Conflict and U.S. Diplomatic Reactions
item.description: The Wall Street Journal’s live coverage focuses on escalating military and diplomatic tensions between Iran and several Western powers in early 2026. Reports indicate that Iran has expanded its missil...

item.title: Calls to reform UK driving laws after confusion over careless and dangerous driving cases
item.description: The article argues that the UK’s current framework for prosecuting fatal driving cases is too vague, inconsistent and poorly understood. Drawing on two recent court cases, the author says the differen...

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith Charged with War Crimes Following Investigation
item.description: Former Australian Special Air Service (SAS) soldier Ben Roberts-Smith has been arrested and charged with war crimes, marking a significant development in a long-running investigation into allegations ...

item.title: Sharon Wambui's Journey from Logistics to Mitumba: How She Rebuilt Her Life in Nairobi
item.description: Sharon Wambui Njeri made a bold decision to leave her thriving career in logistics in Canada and return to Nairobi, Kenya, with the goal of tapping into the second-hand clothing business. After spendi...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal Faces New Storm Threat Following Tornado and Flooding
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal residents are facing another bout of severe weather after a recent storm caused widespread damage and a confirmed tornado touched down in Bishopstowe. Following Sunday’s intense storm, t...

item.title: Artemis II astronauts break Apollo 13 distance record during lunar fly-by
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II mission has made history as its crew of four astronauts, including three Americans and one Canadian, became the furthest humans from Earth ever, surpassing the 400,171-kilometre reco...

item.title: Priyadarshan’s ‘Bhooth Bangla’ Trailer Released Featuring Star-Studded Cast
item.description: The much-anticipated trailer of 'Bhooth Bangla', a comedy directed by Priyadarshan, has been released, showcasing Akshay Kumar, Tabu, and Wamiqa Gabbi in a humorous tale set inside a haunted house. Th...

item.title: 1.8 Million-Year-Old Elephant Butchery Evidence Found at Olduvai Gorge, Tanzania
item.description: Scientists have discovered one of the earliest known cases of hominins butchering a giant prehistoric elephant at the EAK site in Olduvai Gorge, Tanzania. The find dates back to about 1.8 million year...

item.title: Pakistan’s Economic and Diplomatic Balancing Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: The article by Arifa Noor discusses Pakistan’s delicate balancing act both at home and abroad during a time of regional war and economic uncertainty. Islamabad is navigating a tightrope of domestic in...

item.title: Human Ambitions and the Moon: From Culture to Space Colonisation
item.description: Since ancient times, humans have been mesmerised by the moon, which has inspired myths, folklore, and poetry in many cultures, including the Indo-Persian tradition. The moon influences not just tides ...

item.title: Graham Arnold celebrated in Sydney after leading Iraq to World Cup qualification
item.description: Graham Arnold, former coach of the Socceroos, has returned to Sydney to a warm reception after guiding Iraq to qualify for the FIFA World Cup for the first time in 40 years. Arnold took over as Iraq's...

item.title: Trump’s Aggressive Stance on Iran: Threats and Diplomatic Efforts
item.description: US President Donald Trump has been issuing aggressive threats towards Iran, warning that the country could be destroyed in 'one night' if it does not open the Strait of Hormuz by Wednesday. Despite on...

item.title: Gujarat CM urges calm amid international tensions and highlights BJP’s national ideology
item.description: On April 6, 2026, Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel addressed party workers at the Bharatiya Janata Party's 47th Foundation Day celebrations in Gandhinagar. Mr. Patel stressed the BJP’s commitmen...

item.title: Four people die in two separate taxi crashes in Mpumalanga
item.description: In a tragic day on Mpumalanga roads, four people lost their lives in two separate minibus taxi accidents on Monday. The first incident occurred on the N2 between eMkhondo and Ermelo, where a head-on c...

item.title: PTCL’s new fibre optic rollout links landline service to internet bundles in Karachi
item.description: Pakistan Telecommunications Company Limited (PTCL) has started replacing its old copper wire network in Karachi with new fibre optic cables, introducing major changes to its landline system. Many resi...

item.title: Celebrity Easter Celebrations: A Ranking from High-End to High Street
item.description: A recent article has ranked various celebrity Easter celebrations, offering a lighthearted assessment of their extravagance and style. The rankings span a spectrum from overly ostentatious displays to...

item.title: UK Sees Significant Increase in Ebike and E-Scooter Fires Amid Battery Safety Concerns
item.description: The UK experienced a record rise in fires involving electric bicycles (ebikes) and e-scooters in 2025, with at least 432 ebike fires and 147 e-scooter fires recorded. This represents a 38% increase fo...

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Reaches Record Distance from Earth During Lunar Flyby
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II mission has set a new milestone in human space exploration by surpassing the previous record for distance from Earth held by Apollo 13 in 1970. On April 6, 2026, astronauts Reid Wise...
